claim_boundary: Followup366 is an increment-gate and ROCm-lane status receipt, now amended with followup370 native HIP residual/JVP replay, followup372 structural load-path gate, followup373 resident HIP process-worker status, and followup374 frame-material local constitutive FD consistency. It does not create a new restart checkpoint; followup365 remains the retained residual-gate frontier.

followup366_increment_probe.assessment: The followup365 residual-gate checkpoint is already below tolerance. Forcing an additional attached-policy Newton correction with relative-increment convergence required produced no residual descent, so no new increment-gate checkpoint is promoted.

followup370_native_hip_full_residual_bat.assessment: Followup370 validates native HIP full residual/JVP batch replay for the retained attached-policy gate checkpoint and shows useful kernel speedup. It narrows the engine risk but does not replace the missing persistent Rust/HIP Newton worker; followup372 separately resolves the structural pressure load-path gate.

followup372_structural_load_path_gate.assessment: The legacy all_components replay remains useful negative evidence for two unattached one-element pressure components. The production structural_components_only policy resolves those external-only rows and passes the residual gate on CPU batch and native HIP replay.

followup373_resident_hip_worker_replay.assessment: Followup373 keeps the native HIP full-residual/JVP operator buffers resident across two evaluations in one worker PID. It closes the per-evaluation subprocess residency gap but does not yet provide the final in-process Rust FFI Newton worker.

followup374_material_newton_consistency.assessment: Frame-material local constitutive stress derivatives now match finite-difference tangents for stable states in the representative MGT inventory. This narrows material Newton risk but leaves shell/plate material nonlinearity, fiber-section history variables, and global path-dependent material Newton closure open.

rocm_runtime_status.rocminfo_gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6900 XT / gfx1030 / 80 compute units

torch_rocm.impact: host_ilu_device_gmres profile cannot use the Torch ROCm device solver path in this environment.

rocm_runtime_status.assessment: Native HIP kernels work outside the sandbox and ROCm sees the RX 6900 XT, but Torch ROCm exposes no cuda/HIP device. The production Newton solver lane therefore needs the native HIP/Rust path rather than the current Torch device-GMRES profile.
